reasoning: Haan, look—I came here to vote BJP again, and that's what I'm doing. Shantanu won by a landslide last time, the CAA rules finally came in March, and at least he kept that one promise. But I'm not voting for him because I believe anything will change. I'm voting because going back to TMC feels like admitting I was wrong, and Das is just another durbar face anyway. The truth is, my agency will still pay the same tolls to the same muscle, my contracts will still depend on the right connections, and whether Shantanu or Das wins—Bangaon's system stays rotten. But the CAA came, even if it's just 14 certificates now. At least something happened on paper. So I'm marking BJP, but with no illusions. The durbar continues either way.
primary_drivers: CAA was delivered (however minimally) — at least one promise kept
TMC's local durbar is what pushed me away in 2019; going back feels like defeat
Resignation that Shantanu will win anyway and the system won't change regardless of who I vote for
